one_sentence_question: Approve the Zero-Trust cutover: bind the API to localhost/VPN, add TLS, and gate the read (GET) endpoints with a token?
why_it_needs_you: It changes live security settings and affects phone access, so it is yours to authorize. On approval it is operator-run via the runbook (Claude will not flip security settings itself).
options: Approve — proceed with the cutover per runbook
recommendation_and_why: APPROVE when you have ~10 min: it closes the real SC-7 (0.0.0.0 binding), SC-8 (no TLS) and AC-3/IA-2 (ungated reads) gaps. Fully staged + tested (17 tests). Provision the read token to phone/wall FIRST; reversible in one line.
evidence_sanitized: staged: backend/security/zero_trust/* | tests: tests/test_security_hardening.py 17 PASS | runbook: docs/HELM_ZERO_TRUST_CUTOVER.md | bind-audit: FAIL (0.0.0.0 on :8770,:8000,:8010), no TLS
cost_of_delay: medium — dashboards stay plain-HTTP and ungated on all network interfaces until cutover

one_sentence_question: Accept the 11 acknowledged historical spend-ledger link discontinuities (root cause now fixed) so the security posture can recompute above the 76.9% floor?
why_it_needs_you: Accepting residual historical audit findings is a governance/founder call. On approval HELM recomputes control_posture — no security setting is changed.
options: Approve — accept the 11 historical breaks, recompute posture
recommendation_and_why: APPROVE: root cause is fixed (cross-process flock; 0 breaks under an 8x30 concurrency test), there is ZERO tampering (all 9,352 entry hashes valid), and the 11 historical link-breaks are preserved unchanged + acknowledged in a register. Accepting them lets the score reflect the fix.
evidence_sanitized: register: coordination/council/spend_ledger_discontinuities.json (11 breaks, 0 hash mismatches) | fix: spend_meter.py + hoch_ledger.py flock | test: tests/test_spend_ledger_concurrency.py PASS
cost_of_delay: low — the fix is already live; only the score's reflection of it is waiting
